facts.text: Jordan’s 1830s boom featured three gristmills, three sawmills, a sash factory, a distillery, a clothing shop, five taverns, seven general stores, two drug stores, and five grocery stores.
The Jordan Hotel, originally the Clinton House, opened in 1820 as a modest two‑story building.
Local lore says Jordan got its name from a story told by Bessie Sharp in 1938.
The Erie Canal turned Jordan into a commercial, industrial, and transportation center during the 1830s‑1840s and again in the 1870s‑1880s.
Early twentieth‑century Jordan enjoyed both railroad and trolley service, linking it to larger markets.
Every summer, Jordan hosts the Skaneateles Festival of Music, an Antique Boat Show, and Dickens Christmas with actors in period costume.
